summary: Soundboard recording of a superb 1974 London show – crisp overall sound with occasional drop‑outs, highlighted by an epic Dark Star > Morning Dew and stellar Weather Report Suite.
review: The performance is repeatedly praised as one of the best of ’74, featuring a soaring Dark Star into Morning Dew, flawless Weather Report Suite, Let It Grow and a powerful Stella Blue. The soundboard source is generally clear and well‑balanced, though a few reviewers note brief drop‑outs that slightly mar the first set. Overall the music outweighs any technical hiccups, making this a must‑listen for Deadheads.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive 5‑star comments on musical performance; recording is mostly clear with only minor drop‑out complaints, yielding a strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and well‑balanced soundboard mix; several reviewers mention occasional drop‑outs or brief SQ issues in the first set but overall clarity is high.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds slowly but delivers strong peaks (Peggy‑O, Weather Report Suite, Stella Blue). The second set is a highlight with an outstanding NFA and the monumental Dark Star > Morning Dew sequence; transitions are praised for their relaxed yet exploratory feel.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for his tone, Spanish‑mode phrasing, soaring Tiger solo in Dark Star, and overall vocal strength.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as “on top form”, with a clean, clear bass line that stands out throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for energetic screaming at the end of Not Fade Away and adding chaos during the jam sections.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s Rhodes/keyboard work on Weather Report Suite and Let It Grow is called “perfect” and adds soul to the performance.
